description:
You are assisting a data-science team that has just received a compressed bundle of historical
application logs.  Before modelling can begin, you must isolate every line that represents an
“ERROR” raised by the “DataCleaner” module and create a concise, date-level summary.  All work is
to be done from a normal user account (no sudo).  Follow the requirements exactly so that the
automated grader can verify your results.

Initial material
1. A single archive exists at  
   /home/user/datasets/log_archive.tar.gz  
   The archive’s internal structure is:  

       logs/app1.log  
       logs/app2.log  
       logs/app3.log  

2. Each *.log* file is plain text, UTF-8, and every line follows the exact tab-delimited schema  
   YYYY-MM-DD␉HH:MM:SS␉LEVEL␉MODULE␉MESSAGE  
   (␉ = the literal TAB character, not spaces).

Target artefacts that must exist when you are finished
1. Directory:  /home/user/clean_data  
   • Create it if it does not already exist.

2. Filtered log file:  /home/user/clean_data/DataCleaner_ERRORS.log  
   • Contains only those lines whose LEVEL field is literally “ERROR” **and** whose MODULE field is
     literally “DataCleaner”.  
   • Keep each qualifying line exactly as it appeared in its source file (no trimming, no column
     re-ordering, no extra delimiters).  
   • Preserve the order obtained by concatenating the three source logs in ascending filename
     order (app1.log → app2.log → app3.log), line by line.  
   • Terminate the file with a single newline character (i.e., the very last line also ends with
     “\n”).

3. Summary CSV:  /home/user/clean_data/DataCleaner_ERROR_summary.csv  
   • Two columns, comma-separated, **no spaces**: date,count  
   • The header row is literally:  
       date,count  
   • Each subsequent row shows a unique YYYY-MM-DD date (based on the DATE part of filtered
     records) and the integer count of ERROR lines for that date.  
   • Sort these data rows in ascending chronological order.  
   • End the file with a trailing newline character.

4. (Optional for your own debugging) You may write a processing log of your choice inside
   /home/user/clean_data/, but the grader will only inspect the two files named above.

Hints / expectations
• Untar the archive into a temporary working directory inside /home/user, not into system paths.  
• Use standard command-line tools (grep, awk, sed, cut, sort, uniq, etc.) with regular
  expressions; do not rely on Python or other high-level languages.  
• Remember that the delimiter is a **TAB**.  A reliable regex for the LEVEL field would anchor on a
  TAB before “ERROR” and a TAB after it.  
• Make sure your CSV counts aggregate solely by the date portion (characters 1-10 of each
  timestamp).  
• Before finishing, run `cat -A` or a similar command locally to ensure no stray carriage returns
  or extra spaces have crept into either deliverable.

Completion criteria (what the automated tests look for)
• /home/user/clean_data/ exists.  
• /home/user/clean_data/DataCleaner_ERRORS.log exists, is non-empty, and every line matches the
  regex:  
    ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}\t[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}\tERROR\tDataCleaner\t.*$  
• The number of lines in DataCleaner_ERRORS.log equals the sum of the counts listed in
  DataCleaner_ERROR_summary.csv.  
• /home/user/clean_data/DataCleaner_ERROR_summary.csv has exactly one header row followed by the
  correct, chronologically-sorted date/count pairs.  
• Both files end with a single trailing newline.

If every requirement above is met, the task will be marked as successful.
truth:
<!--
Ground-truth information used by the grading system.  DO NOT reveal anything here to the agent.
-->
Expected filtered log file path: /home/user/clean_data/DataCleaner_ERRORS.log
Expected filtered log file exact contents (including final newline):
2023-07-01 10:05:33\tERROR\tDataCleaner\tNull value encountered
2023-07-03 08:15:44\tERROR\tDataCleaner\tOut-of-range value
2023-07-01 14:22:05\tERROR\tDataCleaner\tNull value encountered
2023-07-03 16:35:55\tERROR\tDataCleaner\tOut-of-range value
2023-07-02 12:34:56\tERROR\tDataCleaner\tNull value encountered
2023-07-04 19:00:00\tERROR\tDataCleaner\tOut-of-range value
\n   <-- single Unix newline terminator

Expected summary CSV path: /home/user/clean_data/DataCleaner_ERROR_summary.csv
Expected summary CSV exact contents (including final newline):
date,count
2023-07-01,2
2023-07-02,1
2023-07-03,2
2023-07-04,1
\n

Archive that is pre-populated in the environment:
/home/user/datasets/log_archive.tar.gz
  └── logs/
       ├── app1.log  (contains 4 lines, with 2 qualifying ERRORs)
       ├── app2.log  (contains 3 lines, with 2 qualifying ERRORs)
       └── app3.log  (contains 4 lines, with 2 qualifying ERRORs)

Full contents of each original log file:

app1.log
2023-07-01 10:02:15\tINFO\tDataLoader\tStarting load
2023-07-01 10:05:33\tERROR\tDataCleaner\tNull value encountered
2023-07-02 11:42:07\tWARN\tDataCleaner\tImputed missing values
2023-07-03 08:15:44\tERROR\tDataCleaner\tOut-of-range value

app2.log
2023-07-01 14:22:05\tERROR\tDataCleaner\tNull value encountered
2023-07-02 15:11:23\tINFO\tDataLoader\tLoad complete
2023-07-03 16:35:55\tERROR\tDataCleaner\tOut-of-range value

app3.log
2023-07-01 09:00:00\tINFO\tSystem\tBoot
2023-07-02 12:34:56\tERROR\tDataCleaner\tNull value encountered
2023-07-04 18:48:10\tERROR\tOtherModule\tSomething bad
2023-07-04 19:00:00\tERROR\tDataCleaner\tOut-of-range value

The grader will UNTAR the archive into a temporary directory to verify that the agent’s
DataCleaner_ERRORS.log lines exist verbatim in the source files and that no extra or missing lines
are present.
